title: Elastic Attention Cores for Scalable Vision Transformers
core_claim: A vision transformer can replace all-to-all patch self-attention with a learned core-periphery attention mechanism that routes information through a small set of core tokens, giving linear-time scaling in image patches while staying competitive on classification and dense vision tasks.
triage_rationale: This looks like a substantive architecture paper with a clear technical claim about reducing ViT attention complexity and an explicit tradeoff mechanism for compute versus accuracy. It is also likely useful for review because it targets a well-known bottleneck in vision transformers and could have both research and discussion value if the reported gains hold.
likely_red_flags: Single-paper abstract claims broad competitiveness without any quantitative results in the triage input
Core-periphery routing may introduce optimization or bottleneck issues not visible from the abstract
Performance claims across classification and dense tasks may depend heavily on benchmark selection
